llcFilesWith: Clerk of Superior Court of the county of the entity's legal domicile
soleProprietorFilesWith: Clerk of Superior Court of the county where the business is chiefly carried on
feeBasis: Each county clerk sets the fee. Verified examples in July 2026: Gwinnett $172 plus a $40 publication fee, DeKalb $174, Dade $170.50. Where the clerk does not collect the publication fee, it is paid to the newspaper directly.
renewalRule: Trade names are not renewed. A new registration is filed when ownership or the registered details change.
publicationRule: Notice of the filing runs once a week for two weeks in the county legal organ, the newspaper that carries the sheriff's advertisements. O.C.G.A. § 10-1-490.
protectionNote: Registration is public notice of who owns the business, not a grant of rights in the name. Gwinnett County's own guidance warns against assuming that registration secures exclusive use of the name.
searchNote: There is no statewide trade name database. Registrations are indexed county by county at each Clerk of Superior Court, so clearing a name in Georgia means checking the counties where conflicts are likely. The Secretary of State's eCorp search does not include trade names.
notes: Registration is due within 30 days of starting to do business under the name, verified by affidavit. Georgia is the most expensive pilot state for a DBA even though the state itself files nothing: the county fee plus publication runs a little over $210 in the Atlanta metro counties we verified, against $25 to $50 for the state-level filings elsewhere.
